rerunReason: Rerun after fixes for DeepSeek capture safety, model-listing coverage, Codex timeout Manager semantics, and final-validation artifact scope.

lint.observation: Completed with no whitespace or conflict-marker diagnostics.

auth_alias.observation: Targeted selector, scheduler, session-affinity, alias, OpenAI-compatible alias-pool, and timeout/cooldown tests passed in sdk/cliproxy/auth.

model_listing.observation: Targeted config/model-listing/auth/API command passed across synthesizer, SDK, registry, API, and auth packages.

test.observation: Full repository tests passed with approved resource-aware concurrency across all packages.

typecheck.observation: Server binary built successfully and the transient test-output artifact was removed.

build_artifact_status.observation: No test-output artifact appeared in git status after the build command.

executor_list.observation: Listed concrete TestDeepSeek and TestOpenAICompat executor tests, including capture, replay, non-DeepSeek pass-through, CountTokens, logging, and stream cases.

model_listing_list.observation: Listed DeepSeek model-listing tests in sdk/cliproxy and internal/api, including config/auth/registry, prefix, negative/removal, non-DeepSeek provider, and API JSON cases.

reviewReports.summary: Pass: commit 1f05bd67 is present on the active pr/codex-hang-failover branch and adequately fixes the non-stream assistant/valid-tool-call eligibility gap plus the streaming SSE-error and incomplete-tool-call cache-commit gaps while preserving normal usage-only chunks and stream output behavior.

Pass: the fix adequately addresses the prior blocker that current HEAD lacked DeepSeek-specific model-listing tests. Evidence reviewed includes sdk/cliproxy/deepseek_model_listing_test.go, internal/api/server_test.go, sdk/cliproxy/service.go, the handoff/transcript skeleton, and commit reachability showing 63eb0bd9 is an ancestor of HEAD.

Reviewed the fix feature record, handoff, transcript skeleton, prior failed review, worker skill, shared-state files, and commit c5b7d27b diff. The implementation addresses the prior blocking issues and should pass scrutiny.

Fail: the fix removes the prior unrelated docs/validation directories and includes rerun validator/build evidence, but it still retains and misclassifies an unrelated Z.AI/OpenCode `.factory/research` artifact from the over-scoped final-validation commit.

blockingIssues.description: The final-validation scope cleanup still retains and misclassifies an unrelated .factory/research/provider-research.md artifact as DeepSeek mission context, even though that tracked file contains Z.AI, VibeProxy, and OpenCode Zen/Go research with no DeepSeek content.
blockingIssues.evidence: Review report cleanup-final-validation-artifact-scope-and-rerun.json flags .factory/library/final-validation-smoke-handoff.md line 28 and .factory/research/provider-research.md lines 3-15; direct inspection confirmed provider-research.md is tracked and contains unrelated Z.AI/VibeProxy/OpenCode research notes.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Remove or split the unrelated .factory/research/provider-research.md artifact from the current DeepSeek validation scope, or update final evidence so any retained research file is explicitly not claimed as DeepSeek context and is justified by current milestone relevance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Broaden artifact-scope guidance to cover all unrelated Factory artifact directories, not only .factory/docs and non-DeepSeek .factory/validation, so workers do not retain unrelated .factory/research artifacts as milestone context.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The cleanup review found .factory/research/provider-research.md was retained and described as DeepSeek mission context even though it contains Z.AI/OpenCode/VibeProxy notes from unrelated work.
